name: Pan-Fried Catfish Fillets
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine soy sauce, 1/2 cup orange juice, and ginger in a bowl. Add the catfish and cover. Place in the refrigerator for 2 hours or up to 6.
2. In a saucepan, heat the remaining cup of orange juice over medium heat until reduced by about half. Remove from heat and whisk in butter. Add grated orange rind and keep warm.
3. Drain the marinade and pat the fillets dry with a towel. Dust with cornstarch and shake off excess.
4. Heat oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat. When hot, add catfish 2 fillets at a time. Fry until golden, about 3 minutes, then turn and fry until done, another 3 to 4 minutes depending on thickness of the fillet.
5. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in the oven while cooking remaining 2 fillets.
6. To serve, drizzle the sauce over catfish, arrange on a platter, and garnish with chopped parsley.
